meta.ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions, Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances
meta.project_subject: Setting up of PMU for CPGRAMS with 6 (4+2 new) resource persons for a period upto 31-3-25
meta.deliverables:
1. Review and streamlining existing categorization /taxonomy and mapping across all Central Ministries/Departments (To be completed in two months from date of award of work order)
2. Data analysis and reports on monthly basis including:
   - Root cause analysis of grievances suggested systemic reform
   - Analysis of feedback
   - Analysis of disposal of Grievances and performance of GROS
   - Any other detailed custom analysis as desired by DARPG
3. Study of Public Grievances System for select Ministries/Departments and States/UTs to understand the flow of grievances and bottlenecks (To be completed in 3 months from date of issue of work order)
4. Category analysis of Grievances for States / UTs /Local bodies for universalization of 7.0 (To be completed in six months from the date of issuance of workorder)
5. Any Other work as assigned by DARPG from time to time

content.full_text: This work order from the Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG) extends approval to the Quality Council of India (QCI) for setting up a Project Management Unit (PMU) for CPGRAMS. The project involves 6 QCI professionals (4 members from 01.09.2024 to 31.03.2025 and 2 additional members from 01.11.2024 to 31.03.2025) at a total cost of Rs. 63,84,000/- excluding taxes. The deliverables include reviewing and streamlining existing categorization and mapping across Central Ministries/Departments, monthly data analysis and reports on grievances, studying the Public Grievances System for select Ministries/States/UTs, and category analysis for universalization. The project has specific completion timelines for each deliverable, with payment milestones spread across the project duration. The work order is effective from 01-9-2024 to 31-3-25.
